evidence.text: flexibility flexibility flexibility flexibility flexibility Great Company
Good pay, good pay and that's about it Good pay
Benefits, paid insurance since day 1. Great environment. A lot of opportunities to grow Great place to work. Great benefits
Good pay, hours arenât bad and the work is not difficult. Good benefits that start at day one Pretty good
good pay, hybrid work schedule Good Pay Poor structure department

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Assistant Brand Manager
evidence.text: You are rewarded for raising your hand and contributing meaningfully, so growth opportunities exist if you want to reach for them. Lots of professional development resources available. Strong leadership. Opportunities for growth
-continued education support -company store -flexible and understanding leadership Great Learning Opportunity
Beautiful workspace, free ice cream, great perks, some people are genuinely good people (though many of the best have left) Shady, Unethical, and Fake Leadership
Name recognition of employer on resume Good colleagues Nice campus in NJ Poor Management & Leadership

Flexible hours which are great if you have family responsibilities. Leadership is very understanding and personable. There are a lot of opportunities for growth if you put in the time. Great Company and leadership
Good work life balance. Well known and respected company in the industry. Good work life balance
Good work-life balance and benefits Good work-life balance

Smart people Exciting brands and opportunities Lots of experience across portfolios, brands, and categories Often times free product, there is a company store for purchase The culture is one that cares if only you ask for help Challenging Flat leadership Smart people, exciting brands, caring culture, very corporate, a bit outdated, work life balance dependent on the role
Good work life balance good work life balance Good place to work
Salary, community, work/life balance, incentives n/a
Good agile culture (if your manager will allow it), flexible work environment, great learning with intelligent people. Buyer Beware

Ability to work from home; flexible hours; new, modern building with game room and free ice cream; sit/stand desks with ergonomic chairs; friendly co-workers; half-days on Friday during summer; end of year bonus; good company values; holiday party Customer Service Analyst

For me,close to home , 2-2-3 schedule gives you plenty of days off .Very fair work environment.I have seen several employees transition from production floor to management once they attained their degree,very diverse work force including management.Unilever does not tolerate unprofessional and unsafe behavior will terminate violators so it is a safe work environment. Job facts
very friendly environment and everybody is really helpful. it was a very nice experience and i chose to leave Ratio analysis, Capital structure, Financial statements and Cash flow, Budgeting
Friendly environment, real marketing school Where to learn the real FMCG marketing
Easy job, pay is very good. Horrible management
purposeful company, respect, integrity, family, safe Sales Director
Global company Prestigious global brands Willing to develop POC faster No boundaries, working 7am-7pm non stop every day
job security, exposure, impact-great place to learn at start of one's career Great place to learn business fundamentals
Good benefits and job security Ok Company
12 hour shifts are not for everyone Good pay good benefits
Never knew what you was walking into Good benefits
Long hours and no time off Engineer

the people are the worst, management does not care about you only the numbers and the long hours will make you want to quit. exceptionally boring work or exceedingly stressful, hardly any in between. bad
Very competitive environment, the company will push you to your breaking point but there is usually not a financial reward at the end of the year for bonus or for your salary increase. Great Company

- Rampant nepotism as it relates to promotions & âmoving up the ladderâ - bad/inappropriate behavior from leaders/employees is ignored & brushed under the rug (demeaning comments, yelling, borderline bullying) - leadership team has absolutely no self awareness or even the baseline of what it takes to be effective leaders - lack of integrity with career development opportunitiesâ your merit means nothin...

- Rampant nepotism as it relates to promotions & âmoving up the ladderâ - bad/inappropriate behavior from leaders/employees is ignored & brushed under the rug (demeaning comments, yelling, borderline bullying) - leadership team has absolutely no self awareness or even the baseline of what it takes to be effective leaders - lack of integrity with career development opportunitiesâ your merit means nothin...
Processes for WoW are not always clearly defined Each team has different dynamics for how work gets done Training is not always structured Networking is a must

- Functions outside of sales/marketing, under resourced - low morale - Pay is low, based on the size of the organization and the level of responsibility - Lots of turn-over in sales/marketing, institutional knowledge is not valued - Removal of administration services that support employees day-to-day needs - Limited opportunity for growth within the department Solid Company, Great Team
no growth no growth no growth No good

1% across the board raise for hourly employees while management receives large bonuses. Interoffice affairs are common place & appear to be the only way to move up the ladder. Place has gone down hill under the direction of the current factory director in VT. Benefits are constantly taken away & headcount is being cut causing people to work in unsafe conditions. Yet management threatens people with termination if th...
inefficient work process management disconnect of high level leadership to execution teams pay is not competitive with remainder of market Job Security
Massive favoritism in management, and HR doesn't do much to help out even when major issues are presented. Saw a couple cases of sexual harassment go nowhere. Manufacturing Supervisor

Need to be a parent to advance up ladder. Always trying to cut cost and rely on new managers with no experience. Politics is everything. Pay is based on a median of industry based on work levels and not role based so the extra degrees or difficulty of role don't help your case. Very noble purposes of sustainability are gone with Hein in charge, all is just a marketing show. Good flexibility, toxic management, poor c...
From the favoritism or discrimination you will have to deal with. You will also have to deal with coworkers who should never have been hired. Awful place
They mandated the jab for US corporate teams in 21-22'. Then in Sept 22 after firing several hundred people they rescinded the requirement. Let go for not taking the jab!

As it is a very big organization sometimes you need to find your own way in the complexity. As the company tries to stay ahead of it's competition (which is great!) sometimes too many changes going on. HRBP
the amount of pay wasnt enough for thr amount of work Good company to work for but not a good work environment
Not the best work environment. A lot of unfairness and favoritism. Certain people get opportunities that other do not. Unilever experience
Negative culture in plant due to recent changes in philosophy Organization does treat folks well
The work life balance doesn't exist depending on which department you're in. Can be good
US headquarters is based in NJ for most employees Incredible company, could not recommend it more.
No work/life balance. Too many trainings and priorities that could be scaled. Good benefits and compensation- long hours
Office site change, layoff, a lot of pressure from upper leadership. Promotion limited. Used to be good company for retire but not anymore
Work life balance is not the best Brand Manager
flexibility flexibility flexibility flexibility flexibility Great Company
Good pay, good pay and that's about it Good pay
Benefits, paid insurance since day 1. Great environment. A lot of opportunities to grow Great place to work. Great benefits
Good pay, hours arenât bad and the work is not difficult. Good benefits that start at day one Pretty good
